actors.name: Marine Le Pen
actors.role: President of the National Rally (Rassemblement National), France
actors.blurb: Marine Le Pen remains a dominant figure in French right-wing politics, driving significant coverage with her May Day speeches and the National Rally's strategic positioning ahead of the 2027 presidential elections. Her rhetoric on nationalism, workers' rights, and criticism of welfare dependency continues to shape the party's narrative and generate media attention.

actors.name: Giorgia Meloni
actors.blurb: Giorgia Meloni continues to be a central figure in European right-wing politics, with her government's policies and public appearances generating widespread coverage. Her May Day visit to PizzAut, a pizzeria employing autistic youth, and her government's housing plan have been key drivers of recent news cycles, alongside her stance on international issues like the Gaza flotilla.

actors.blurb: Donald Trump remains the defining figure of the current news cycle, with his administration's policies, international actions, and personal controversies driving extensive coverage. Key issues include the Iran war, executive orders on retirement savings, and his ongoing feud with media figures like Jimmy Kimmel. His influence extends to domestic and international politics, making him a constant subject of debate.

actors.name: Santiago Abascal
actors.blurb: Santiago Abascal is a key driver of far-right news coverage in Spain, with his party's actions and rhetoric shaping the political landscape. His May Day campaign launch in Jaén, aggressive stance on immigration, and clashes with other parties over policy issues like the Mercosur trade deal have kept Vox in the spotlight. His leadership is central to the party's strategy ahead of regional elections.

actors.name: Péter Magyar
actors.blurb: Péter Magyar has rapidly become a defining figure in Hungarian politics following his party's electoral victory. His appointments, including naming his brother-in-law as justice minister, and his government's plans to address corruption and EU funds have generated significant coverage. His actions signal a shift in Hungary's political landscape, with implications for both domestic and international policy.
signals: Anti-corruption and rule of law reforms

actors.name: Jordan Bardella
actors.role: President of the National Rally (Rassemblement National), France
actors.blurb: Jordan Bardella is a rising star in French far-right politics, often sharing the spotlight with Marine Le Pen. His relationship with Princess Maria-Carolina of Bourbon-Two-Sicilies, his stance on issues like taxing TotalEnergies' profits, and his role in the National Rally's May Day events have kept him in the news. His leadership is seen as pivotal for the party's future, particularly ahead of the 2027 presidential elections.
signals: May Day speeches and party events

actors.name: Sylvi Listhaug
actors.role: Leader of the Progress Party (Fremskrittspartiet), Norway
actors.blurb: Sylvi Listhaug has been a prominent figure in Norwegian politics, with her leadership of the Progress Party driving significant coverage. Her speeches at the party's national convention, criticism of the Labour Party, and advocacy for lower taxes and stricter immigration policies have kept her in the spotlight. Her influence is particularly notable ahead of Norway's upcoming elections.
signals: Tax reduction and economic freedom advocacy

His role in shaping Poland's political and legal landscape remains a focal point of coverage.
